message:
refactor(http): consolidate header line parsing into shared byte-level implementation

The HTTP/1.1 codec had two nearly-identical header parsing paths:
`parse_header_line(&str)` and `parse_header_line_bytes(&[u8])`. The
bytes variant simply converted to str first, then called the str variant,
meaning any byte-level validation happened after a potentially-expensive
UTF-8 check.

This commit introduces `parse_header_line_impl(&[u8]) -> Result<(&[u8], &[u8])>`
as the single source of truth for header parsing logic. Both public
functions now delegate to it, performing UTF-8 validation only on the
already-extracted name and value slices rather than the entire line.

Additional changes:
- Extracted `is_valid_header_name_byte(u8) -> bool` as an inline helper,
  replacing the closure in `is_valid_header_name_bytes()` and the match
  in the former `parse_header_line()`. This deduplicates the token-char
  validation table.
- Added an explicit empty-name check (`raw_name.is_empty()`) that was
  previously unreachable due to the colon-position logic but is now
  clearly guarded.
- Value validation (CR/LF rejection) now uses byte iteration
  (`any(|&b| b == b'\r' || b == b'\n')`) instead of str methods,
  matching the byte-first approach.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
